title: Inverse metabolic engineering based on metabonomics for efficient production of hydroxytyrosol by Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
author_string: Liu, Yingjie, Gu, Bixuan, Shi, Jiahua, Fu, Changchun, Zhang, Xuepeng et al.
abstract: Metabolic engineering provides a powerful approach to efficiently produce valuable compounds, with the aid of emerging gene editing tools and diverse metabolic regulation strategies. However, apart from the current known biochemical pathway information, a variety of unclear constraints commonly limited the optimization space of cell phenotype. Hydroxytyrosol is an important phenolic compound that serves various industries with prominent health-beneficial properties. In this study, the inverse metabolic engineering based on metabolome analysis was customized and implemented to disclose the hidden rate-limiting steps and thus to improve hydroxytyrosol production in Saccharomyces cerevisiae (S. cerevisiae). The potential rate-limiting steps involved three modules that were eliminated individually via reinforcing and balancing metabolic flow, optimizing cofactor supply, and weakening the competitive pathways. Ultimately, a 118.53 % improvement in hydroxytyrosol production (639.84 mg/L) was achieved by inverse metabolic engineering.
